'長野県埴科郡坂城町坂城1205', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Sakaki-jinja (Sakaki)}} [[JA:坂城神社]] {{wikidata link|Q106852481}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Sakaki Shrine |画像 = [[File:Sakaki-jinja (Sakaki), keidai.jpg|250px]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Nagano Prefecture|Q127877|WD=Q127877}}{{ill|lt=Hanishina District|Q726661|WD=Q726661}}{{ill|lt=Sakaki|Q1348962|WD=Q1348962}}Sakaki 1205 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto}} |社格 = {{ill||lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} (small)
former {{ill||lt=kensha|UNKNOWN}} |創建 = (trad.) year 40 of 12th emperor {{ill|lt=Emperor Keikō|Q329723|WD=Q329723}} |本殿 = |別名 = Sakaki Ōmiya |札所等 = |例祭 = Third Sunday of April (spring festival)
Fourth Sunday of September (autumn festival) |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nagano|label=Sakaki Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Sakaki-jinja (Sakaki), otorii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Great torii}}]] '''Sakaki Shrine''' (さかきじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Sakaki, {{ill|lt=Sakaki|Q1348962|WD=Q1348962}}, {{ill|lt=Hanishina District|Q726661|WD=Q726661}}, {{ill|lt=Nagano Prefecture|Q127877|WD=Q127877}}. It is a {{ill||lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}}, and its former rank in the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}} was {{ill||lt=kensha|UNKNOWN}}. === Overview === The main deity is {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto}}. Shrine tradition says that in year 40 of {{ill|lt=Emperor Keikō|Q329723|WD=Q329723}}, {{ill|lt=Yamato Takeru|Q461258|WD=Q461258}} enshrined his ancestral deity here during the eastern expedition, and in {{ill|lt=Hakuhō|Q11580612|WD=Q11580612}} 2 ({{ill|lt=673|Q31105|WD=Q31105}}) shrine buildings were dedicated to pray for good governance and peace under heaven. In the {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=register of deities}} of the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}}', completed in {{ill|lt=Enchō (era)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Enchō}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470}}), it is listed as "Sakaki Shrine" in {{ill|lt=Hanishina District|Q726661|WD=Q726661}}, {{ill|lt=Shinano Province|Q289250|WD=Q289250}}, and ranked as {{ill||lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}}. It was worshiped as the village shrine of Sakaki-gō, Hanishina District, and from the medieval period onward was called "Sakaki Ōmiya." It was revered by generations of the {{ill|lt=Murakami clan|Q11522833|WD=Q11522833}}, lords of {{ill|lt=Katsurao Castle|Q11620930|WD=Q11620930}}, and the site includes Mansen-ji, the remains of the Murakami residence, adjacent to the precincts. Katsurao Castle fell to the {{ill|lt=Takeda clan|Q1210114|WD=Q1210114}} in {{ill|lt=Tenbun (era)|Q1192853|WD=Q1192853|lt=Tenbun}} 22 ({{ill|lt=1553|Q6568|WD=Q6568}}), but in {{ill|lt=Eiroku|Q1143341|WD=Q1143341}} 11 ({{ill|lt=1568|Q6648|WD=Q6648}}), {{ill|lt=Takeda Shingen|Q276404|WD=Q276404}} donated shrine lands and revived it, and {{ill|lt=Takeda Katsuyori|Q662482|WD=Q662482}} also guaranteed those lands in {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058}} 7 ({{ill|lt=1579|Q6709|WD=Q6709}}). It is also said to have been greatly revered by magistrates of the {{ill|lt=Nakanojō Jinya|Q21653911|WD=Q21653911}} in the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963}}. === Precinct === File:Sakaki-jinja (Sakaki), honden.jpg|Honden File:Sakaki-jinja (Sakaki), haiden.jpg|Haiden File:Sakaki-jinja (Sakaki), komainu-2.jpg|Komainu (A), Sakaki Town designated cultural property File:Sakaki-jinja (Sakaki), komainu-3.jpg|Komainu (Un), Sakaki Town designated cultural property File:Sakaki-jinja (Sakaki), torii.jpg|Precinct torii File:Sakaki-jinja (Sakaki), entrance.jpg|Precinct entrance === Cultural properties === === Sakaki Town designated cultural properties === * Tangible cultural properties ** Wooden Kishin mask (sculpture) - designated October 1, 1984 (Shōwa 59). ** Wooden lion head (sculpture) - designated October 1, 1984 (Shōwa 59). ** One pair of Sakaki Shrine komainu (sculpture) - designated April 1, 1999 (Heisei 11). ** Four old documents of Sakaki Shrine (old documents) - donation letter of shrine lands by Takeda Shingen, guarantee letter by Takeda Katsuyori, prohibition by Hasegawa Yasazaemon, donation letter of sacred lands by Murakami Yoshikiyo. Designated October 1, 1984 (Shōwa 59). === Bibliography === *'Shinshu Culture Series: Temples and Shrines,' 1981 {{ill|lt=Shinano Mainichi Shimbun|Q11386181|WD=Q11386181|lt=Shinano Mainichi Shimbun Co.}} *Nagano Prefectural Shrine Association (supervision), 'Rituals of Shinshu,' Ginga Shobō, 1990 *Edited by {{ill|lt=Kenichi Tanigawa|Q2392596|WD=Q2392596}}, 'Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Places - 9: Mino, Hida, Shinano,' 2000 {{ill|lt=Hakusuisha|Q4346470|WD=Q4346470}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === * {{Commonscat-inline|Sakaki-jinja (Sakaki)}} {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=長野県}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:さかきしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sakaki Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sakaki Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shinano Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:長野県の神社]] [[Category:坂城町の建築物]] [[Category:坂城町の歴史]] [[Category:信濃国の式内社]] [[Category:長野県の旧県社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

'長野県埴科郡坂城町坂城1205', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Sakaki-jinja (Sakaki)}} [[JA:坂城神社]] {{wikidata link|Q106852481}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Sakaki Shrine |画像 = [[File:Sakaki-jinja (Sakaki), keidai.jpg|250px]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Nagano Prefecture|Q127877|WD=Q127877}}{{ill|lt=Hanishina District|Q726661|WD=Q726661}}{{ill|lt=Sakaki|Q1348962|WD=Q1348962}}Sakaki 1205 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} (small)
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|UNKNOWN}} |創建 = (trad.) year 40 of 12th emperor {{ill|lt=Emperor Keikō|Q329723|WD=Q329723}} |本殿 = |別名 = Sakaki Ōmiya |札所等 = |例祭 = Third Sunday of April (spring festival)
Fourth Sunday of September (autumn festival) |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nagano|label=Sakaki Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Sakaki-jinja (Sakaki), otorii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Great torii}}]] '''Sakaki Shrine''' (さかきじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Sakaki, {{ill|lt=Sakaki|Q1348962|WD=Q1348962}}, {{ill|lt=Hanishina District|Q726661|WD=Q726661}}, {{ill|lt=Nagano Prefecture|Q127877|WD=Q127877}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}}, and its former rank in the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}} was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|UNKNOWN}}. === Overview === The main deity is {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto}}. Shrine tradition says that in year 40 of {{ill|lt=Emperor Keikō|Q329723|WD=Q329723}}, {{ill|lt=Yamato Takeru|Q461258|WD=Q461258}} enshrined his ancestral deity here during the eastern expedition, and in {{ill|lt=Hakuhō|Q11580612|WD=Q11580612}} 2 ({{ill|lt=673|Q31105|WD=Q31105}}) shrine buildings were dedicated to pray for good governance and peace under heaven. In the {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=register of deities}} of the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}}', completed in {{ill|lt=Enchō (era)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Enchō}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470}}), it is listed as "Sakaki Shrine" in {{ill|lt=Hanishina District|Q726661|WD=Q726661}}, {{ill|lt=Shinano Province|Q289250|WD=Q289250}}, and ranked as {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}}. It was worshiped as the village shrine of Sakaki-gō, Hanishina District, and from the medieval period onward was called "Sakaki Ōmiya." It was revered by generations of the {{ill|lt=Murakami clan|Q11522833|WD=Q11522833}}, lords of {{ill|lt=Katsurao Castle|Q11620930|WD=Q11620930}}, and the site includes Mansen-ji, the remains of the Murakami residence, adjacent to the precincts. Katsurao Castle fell to the {{ill|lt=Takeda clan|Q1210114|WD=Q1210114}} in {{ill|lt=Tenbun (era)|Q1192853|WD=Q1192853|lt=Tenbun}} 22 ({{ill|lt=1553|Q6568|WD=Q6568}}), but in {{ill|lt=Eiroku|Q1143341|WD=Q1143341}} 11 ({{ill|lt=1568|Q6648|WD=Q6648}}), {{ill|lt=Takeda Shingen|Q276404|WD=Q276404}} donated shrine lands and revived it, and {{ill|lt=Takeda Katsuyori|Q662482|WD=Q662482}} also guaranteed those lands in {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058}} 7 ({{ill|lt=1579|Q6709|WD=Q6709}}). It is also said to have been greatly revered by magistrates of the {{ill|lt=Nakanojō Jinya|Q21653911|WD=Q21653911}} in the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963}}. === Precinct === File:Sakaki-jinja (Sakaki), honden.jpg|Honden File:Sakaki-jinja (Sakaki), haiden.jpg|Haiden File:Sakaki-jinja (Sakaki), komainu-2.jpg|Komainu (A), Sakaki Town designated cultural property File:Sakaki-jinja (Sakaki), komainu-3.jpg|Komainu (Un), Sakaki Town designated cultural property File:Sakaki-jinja (Sakaki), torii.jpg|Precinct torii File:Sakaki-jinja (Sakaki), entrance.jpg|Precinct entrance === Cultural properties === === Sakaki Town designated cultural properties === * Tangible cultural properties ** Wooden Kishin mask (sculpture) - designated October 1, 1984 (Shōwa 59). ** Wooden lion head (sculpture) - designated October 1, 1984 (Shōwa 59). ** One pair of Sakaki Shrine komainu (sculpture) - designated April 1, 1999 (Heisei 11). ** Four old documents of Sakaki Shrine (old documents) - donation letter of shrine lands by Takeda Shingen, guarantee letter by Takeda Katsuyori, prohibition by Hasegawa Yasazaemon, donation letter of sacred lands by Murakami Yoshikiyo. Designated October 1, 1984 (Shōwa 59). === Bibliography === *'Shinshu Culture Series: Temples and Shrines,' 1981 {{ill|lt=Shinano Mainichi Shimbun|Q11386181|WD=Q11386181|lt=Shinano Mainichi Shimbun Co.}} *Nagano Prefectural Shrine Association (supervision), 'Rituals of Shinshu,' Ginga Shobō, 1990 *Edited by {{ill|lt=Kenichi Tanigawa|Q2392596|WD=Q2392596}}, 'Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Places - 9: Mino, Hida, Shinano,' 2000 {{ill|lt=Hakusuisha|Q4346470|WD=Q4346470}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === * {{Commonscat-inline|Sakaki-jinja (Sakaki)}} {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=長野県}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:さかきしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sakaki Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sakaki Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shinano Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:長野県の神社]] [[Category:坂城町の建築物]] [[Category:坂城町の歴史]] [[Category:信濃国の式内社]] [[Category:長野県の旧県社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

'長野県埴科郡坂城町坂城1205', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Sakaki-jinja (Sakaki)}} [[JA:坂城神社]] {{wikidata link|Q106852481}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Sakaki Shrine |画像 = [[File:Sakaki-jinja (Sakaki), keidai.jpg|250px]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Nagano Prefecture|Q127877|WD=Q127877}}{{ill|lt=Hanishina District|Q726661|WD=Q726661}}{{ill|lt=Sakaki|Q1348962|WD=Q1348962}}Sakaki 1205 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}} (small)
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=UNKNOWN}} |創建 = (trad.) year 40 of 12th emperor {{ill|lt=Emperor Keikō|Q329723|WD=Q329723}} |本殿 = |別名 = Sakaki Ōmiya |札所等 = |例祭 = Third Sunday of April (spring festival)
Fourth Sunday of September (autumn festival) |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nagano|label=Sakaki Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Sakaki-jinja (Sakaki), otorii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Great torii}}]] '''Sakaki Shrine''' (さかきじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Sakaki, {{ill|lt=Sakaki|Q1348962|WD=Q1348962}}, {{ill|lt=Hanishina District|Q726661|WD=Q726661}}, {{ill|lt=Nagano Prefecture|Q127877|WD=Q127877}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}}, and its former rank in the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}} was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=UNKNOWN}}. === Overview === The main deity is {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto}}. Shrine tradition says that in year 40 of {{ill|lt=Emperor Keikō|Q329723|WD=Q329723}}, {{ill|lt=Yamato Takeru|Q461258|WD=Q461258}} enshrined his ancestral deity here during the eastern expedition, and in {{ill|lt=Hakuhō|Q11580612|WD=Q11580612}} 2 ({{ill|lt=673|Q31105|WD=Q31105}}) shrine buildings were dedicated to pray for good governance and peace under heaven. In the {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=register of deities}} of the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}}', completed in {{ill|lt=Enchō (era)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Enchō}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470}}), it is listed as "Sakaki Shrine" in {{ill|lt=Hanishina District|Q726661|WD=Q726661}}, {{ill|lt=Shinano Province|Q289250|WD=Q289250}}, and ranked as {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}}. It was worshiped as the village shrine of Sakaki-gō, Hanishina District, and from the medieval period onward was called "Sakaki Ōmiya." It was revered by generations of the {{ill|lt=Murakami clan|Q11522833|WD=Q11522833}}, lords of {{ill|lt=Katsurao Castle|Q11620930|WD=Q11620930}}, and the site includes Mansen-ji, the remains of the Murakami residence, adjacent to the precincts. Katsurao Castle fell to the {{ill|lt=Takeda clan|Q1210114|WD=Q1210114}} in {{ill|lt=Tenbun (era)|Q1192853|WD=Q1192853|lt=Tenbun}} 22 ({{ill|lt=1553|Q6568|WD=Q6568}}), but in {{ill|lt=Eiroku|Q1143341|WD=Q1143341}} 11 ({{ill|lt=1568|Q6648|WD=Q6648}}), {{ill|lt=Takeda Shingen|Q276404|WD=Q276404}} donated shrine lands and revived it, and {{ill|lt=Takeda Katsuyori|Q662482|WD=Q662482}} also guaranteed those lands in {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058}} 7 ({{ill|lt=1579|Q6709|WD=Q6709}}). It is also said to have been greatly revered by magistrates of the {{ill|lt=Nakanojō Jinya|Q21653911|WD=Q21653911}} in the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963}}. === Precinct === File:Sakaki-jinja (Sakaki), honden.jpg|Honden File:Sakaki-jinja (Sakaki), haiden.jpg|Haiden File:Sakaki-jinja (Sakaki), komainu-2.jpg|Komainu (A), Sakaki Town designated cultural property File:Sakaki-jinja (Sakaki), komainu-3.jpg|Komainu (Un), Sakaki Town designated cultural property File:Sakaki-jinja (Sakaki), torii.jpg|Precinct torii File:Sakaki-jinja (Sakaki), entrance.jpg|Precinct entrance === Cultural properties === === Sakaki Town designated cultural properties === * Tangible cultural properties ** Wooden Kishin mask (sculpture) - designated October 1, 1984 (Shōwa 59). ** Wooden lion head (sculpture) - designated October 1, 1984 (Shōwa 59). ** One pair of Sakaki Shrine komainu (sculpture) - designated April 1, 1999 (Heisei 11). ** Four old documents of Sakaki Shrine (old documents) - donation letter of shrine lands by Takeda Shingen, guarantee letter by Takeda Katsuyori, prohibition by Hasegawa Yasazaemon, donation letter of sacred lands by Murakami Yoshikiyo. Designated October 1, 1984 (Shōwa 59). === Bibliography === *'Shinshu Culture Series: Temples and Shrines,' 1981 {{ill|lt=Shinano Mainichi Shimbun|Q11386181|WD=Q11386181|lt=Shinano Mainichi Shimbun Co.}} *Nagano Prefectural Shrine Association (supervision), 'Rituals of Shinshu,' Ginga Shobō, 1990 *Edited by {{ill|lt=Kenichi Tanigawa|Q2392596|WD=Q2392596}}, 'Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Places - 9: Mino, Hida, Shinano,' 2000 {{ill|lt=Hakusuisha|Q4346470|WD=Q4346470}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === * {{Commonscat-inline|Sakaki-jinja (Sakaki)}} {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=長野県}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:さかきしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sakaki Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sakaki Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shinano Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:長野県の神社]] [[Category:坂城町の建築物]] [[Category:坂城町の歴史]] [[Category:信濃国の式内社]] [[Category:長野県の旧県社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

'長野県埴科郡坂城町坂城1205', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Sakaki-jinja (Sakaki)}} [[JA:坂城神社]] {{wikidata link|Q106852481}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Sakaki Shrine |画像 = [[File:Sakaki-jinja (Sakaki), keidai.jpg|250px]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Nagano Prefecture|Q127877|WD=Q127877}}{{ill|lt=Hanishina District|Q726661|WD=Q726661}}{{ill|lt=Sakaki|Q1348962|WD=Q1348962}}Sakaki 1205 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=UNKNOWN}} |創建 = (trad.) year 40 of 12th emperor {{ill|lt=Emperor Keikō|Q329723|WD=Q329723}} |本殿 = |別名 = Sakaki Ōmiya |札所等 = |例祭 = Third Sunday of April (spring festival)
Fourth Sunday of September (autumn festival) |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nagano|label=Sakaki Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Sakaki-jinja (Sakaki), otorii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Great torii}}]] '''Sakaki Shrine''' (さかきじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Sakaki, {{ill|lt=Sakaki|Q1348962|WD=Q1348962}}, {{ill|lt=Hanishina District|Q726661|WD=Q726661}}, {{ill|lt=Nagano Prefecture|Q127877|WD=Q127877}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former rank in the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}} was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=UNKNOWN}}. === Overview === The main deity is {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto}}. Shrine tradition says that in year 40 of {{ill|lt=Emperor Keikō|Q329723|WD=Q329723}}, {{ill|lt=Yamato Takeru|Q461258|WD=Q461258}} enshrined his ancestral deity here during the eastern expedition, and in {{ill|lt=Hakuhō|Q11580612|WD=Q11580612}} 2 ({{ill|lt=673|Q31105|WD=Q31105}}) shrine buildings were dedicated to pray for good governance and peace under heaven. In the {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=register of deities}} of the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}}', completed in {{ill|lt=Enchō (era)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Enchō}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470}}), it is listed as "Sakaki Shrine" in {{ill|lt=Hanishina District|Q726661|WD=Q726661}}, {{ill|lt=Shinano Province|Q289250|WD=Q289250}}, and ranked as {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}. It was worshiped as the village shrine of Sakaki-gō, Hanishina District, and from the medieval period onward was called "Sakaki Ōmiya." It was revered by generations of the {{ill|lt=Murakami clan|Q11522833|WD=Q11522833}}, lords of {{ill|lt=Katsurao Castle|Q11620930|WD=Q11620930}}, and the site includes Mansen-ji, the remains of the Murakami residence, adjacent to the precincts. Katsurao Castle fell to the {{ill|lt=Takeda clan|Q1210114|WD=Q1210114}} in {{ill|lt=Tenbun (era)|Q1192853|WD=Q1192853|lt=Tenbun}} 22 ({{ill|lt=1553|Q6568|WD=Q6568}}), but in {{ill|lt=Eiroku|Q1143341|WD=Q1143341}} 11 ({{ill|lt=1568|Q6648|WD=Q6648}}), {{ill|lt=Takeda Shingen|Q276404|WD=Q276404}} donated shrine lands and revived it, and {{ill|lt=Takeda Katsuyori|Q662482|WD=Q662482}} also guaranteed those lands in {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058}} 7 ({{ill|lt=1579|Q6709|WD=Q6709}}). It is also said to have been greatly revered by magistrates of the {{ill|lt=Nakanojō Jinya|Q21653911|WD=Q21653911}} in the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963}}. === Precinct === File:Sakaki-jinja (Sakaki), honden.jpg|Honden File:Sakaki-jinja (Sakaki), haiden.jpg|Haiden File:Sakaki-jinja (Sakaki), komainu-2.jpg|Komainu (A), Sakaki Town designated cultural property File:Sakaki-jinja (Sakaki), komainu-3.jpg|Komainu (Un), Sakaki Town designated cultural property File:Sakaki-jinja (Sakaki), torii.jpg|Precinct torii File:Sakaki-jinja (Sakaki), entrance.jpg|Precinct entrance === Cultural properties === === Sakaki Town designated cultural properties === * Tangible cultural properties ** Wooden Kishin mask (sculpture) - designated October 1, 1984 (Shōwa 59). ** Wooden lion head (sculpture) - designated October 1, 1984 (Shōwa 59). ** One pair of Sakaki Shrine komainu (sculpture) - designated April 1, 1999 (Heisei 11). ** Four old documents of Sakaki Shrine (old documents) - donation letter of shrine lands by Takeda Shingen, guarantee letter by Takeda Katsuyori, prohibition by Hasegawa Yasazaemon, donation letter of sacred lands by Murakami Yoshikiyo. Designated October 1, 1984 (Shōwa 59). === Bibliography === *'Shinshu Culture Series: Temples and Shrines,' 1981 {{ill|lt=Shinano Mainichi Shimbun|Q11386181|WD=Q11386181|lt=Shinano Mainichi Shimbun Co.}} *Nagano Prefectural Shrine Association (supervision), 'Rituals of Shinshu,' Ginga Shobō, 1990 *Edited by {{ill|lt=Kenichi Tanigawa|Q2392596|WD=Q2392596}}, 'Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Places - 9: Mino, Hida, Shinano,' 2000 {{ill|lt=Hakusuisha|Q4346470|WD=Q4346470}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === * {{Commonscat-inline|Sakaki-jinja (Sakaki)}} {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=長野県}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:さかきしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sakaki Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sakaki Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shinano Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:長野県の神社]] [[Category:坂城町の建築物]] [[Category:坂城町の歴史]] [[Category:信濃国の式内社]] [[Category:長野県の旧県社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

'長野県埴科郡坂城町坂城1205', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Sakaki-jinja (Sakaki)}} [[JA:坂城神社]] {{wikidata link|Q106852481}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Sakaki Shrine |画像 = [[File:Sakaki-jinja (Sakaki), keidai.jpg|250px]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Nagano Prefecture|Q127877|WD=Q127877}}{{ill|lt=Hanishina District|Q726661|WD=Q726661}}{{ill|lt=Sakaki|Q1348962|WD=Q1348962}}Sakaki 1205 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q90139226}} |創建 = (trad.) year 40 of 12th emperor {{ill|lt=Emperor Keikō|Q329723|WD=Q329723}} |本殿 = |別名 = Sakaki Ōmiya |札所等 = |例祭 = Third Sunday of April (spring festival)
Fourth Sunday of September (autumn festival) |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nagano|label=Sakaki Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Sakaki-jinja (Sakaki), otorii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Great torii}}]] '''Sakaki Shrine''' (さかきじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Sakaki, {{ill|lt=Sakaki|Q1348962|WD=Q1348962}}, {{ill|lt=Hanishina District|Q726661|WD=Q726661}}, {{ill|lt=Nagano Prefecture|Q127877|WD=Q127877}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former rank in the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}} was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q90139226}}. === Overview === The main deity is {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto}}. Shrine tradition says that in year 40 of {{ill|lt=Emperor Keikō|Q329723|WD=Q329723}}, {{ill|lt=Yamato Takeru|Q461258|WD=Q461258}} enshrined his ancestral deity here during the eastern expedition, and in {{ill|lt=Hakuhō|Q11580612|WD=Q11580612}} 2 ({{ill|lt=673|Q31105|WD=Q31105}}) shrine buildings were dedicated to pray for good governance and peace under heaven. In the {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=register of deities}} of the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}}', completed in {{ill|lt=Enchō (era)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Enchō}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470}}), it is listed as "Sakaki Shrine" in {{ill|lt=Hanishina District|Q726661|WD=Q726661}}, {{ill|lt=Shinano Province|Q289250|WD=Q289250}}, and ranked as {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}. It was worshiped as the village shrine of Sakaki-gō, Hanishina District, and from the medieval period onward was called "Sakaki Ōmiya." It was revered by generations of the {{ill|lt=Murakami clan|Q11522833|WD=Q11522833}}, lords of {{ill|lt=Katsurao Castle|Q11620930|WD=Q11620930}}, and the site includes Mansen-ji, the remains of the Murakami residence, adjacent to the precincts. Katsurao Castle fell to the {{ill|lt=Takeda clan|Q1210114|WD=Q1210114}} in {{ill|lt=Tenbun (era)|Q1192853|WD=Q1192853|lt=Tenbun}} 22 ({{ill|lt=1553|Q6568|WD=Q6568}}), but in {{ill|lt=Eiroku|Q1143341|WD=Q1143341}} 11 ({{ill|lt=1568|Q6648|WD=Q6648}}), {{ill|lt=Takeda Shingen|Q276404|WD=Q276404}} donated shrine lands and revived it, and {{ill|lt=Takeda Katsuyori|Q662482|WD=Q662482}} also guaranteed those lands in {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058}} 7 ({{ill|lt=1579|Q6709|WD=Q6709}}). It is also said to have been greatly revered by magistrates of the {{ill|lt=Nakanojō Jinya|Q21653911|WD=Q21653911}} in the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963}}. === Precinct === File:Sakaki-jinja (Sakaki), honden.jpg|Honden File:Sakaki-jinja (Sakaki), haiden.jpg|Haiden File:Sakaki-jinja (Sakaki), komainu-2.jpg|Komainu (A), Sakaki Town designated cultural property File:Sakaki-jinja (Sakaki), komainu-3.jpg|Komainu (Un), Sakaki Town designated cultural property File:Sakaki-jinja (Sakaki), torii.jpg|Precinct torii File:Sakaki-jinja (Sakaki), entrance.jpg|Precinct entrance === Cultural properties === === Sakaki Town designated cultural properties === * Tangible cultural properties ** Wooden Kishin mask (sculpture) - designated October 1, 1984 (Shōwa 59). ** Wooden lion head (sculpture) - designated October 1, 1984 (Shōwa 59). ** One pair of Sakaki Shrine komainu (sculpture) - designated April 1, 1999 (Heisei 11). ** Four old documents of Sakaki Shrine (old documents) - donation letter of shrine lands by Takeda Shingen, guarantee letter by Takeda Katsuyori, prohibition by Hasegawa Yasazaemon, donation letter of sacred lands by Murakami Yoshikiyo. Designated October 1, 1984 (Shōwa 59). === Bibliography === *'Shinshu Culture Series: Temples and Shrines,' 1981 {{ill|lt=Shinano Mainichi Shimbun|Q11386181|WD=Q11386181|lt=Shinano Mainichi Shimbun Co.}} *Nagano Prefectural Shrine Association (supervision), 'Rituals of Shinshu,' Ginga Shobō, 1990 *Edited by {{ill|lt=Kenichi Tanigawa|Q2392596|WD=Q2392596}}, 'Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Places - 9: Mino, Hida, Shinano,' 2000 {{ill|lt=Hakusuisha|Q4346470|WD=Q4346470}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === * {{Commonscat-inline|Sakaki-jinja (Sakaki)}} {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=長野県}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:さかきしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sakaki Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sakaki Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shinano Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:長野県の神社]] [[Category:坂城町の建築物]] [[Category:坂城町の歴史]] [[Category:信濃国の式内社]] [[Category:長野県の旧県社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

'長野県埴科郡坂城町坂城1205', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Sakaki-jinja (Sakaki)}} [[JA:坂城神社]] {{wikidata link|Q106852481}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Sakaki Shrine |画像 = [[File:Sakaki-jinja (Sakaki), keidai.jpg|250px]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Nagano Prefecture|Q127877|WD=Q127877}}{{ill|lt=Hanishina District|Q726661|WD=Q726661}}{{ill|lt=Sakaki|Q1348962|WD=Q1348962}}Sakaki 1205 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q90139226}} |創建 = (trad.) year 40 of 12th emperor {{ill|lt=Emperor Keikō|Q329723|WD=Q329723}} |本殿 = |別名 = Sakaki Ōmiya |札所等 = |例祭 = Third Sunday of April (spring festival)
Fourth Sunday of September (autumn festival) |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nagano|label=Sakaki Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Sakaki-jinja (Sakaki), otorii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Great torii}}]] '''Sakaki Shrine''' (さかきじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Sakaki, {{ill|lt=Sakaki|Q1348962|WD=Q1348962}}, {{ill|lt=Hanishina District|Q726661|WD=Q726661}}, {{ill|lt=Nagano Prefecture|Q127877|WD=Q127877}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former rank in the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}} was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q90139226}}. === Overview === The main deity is {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto}}. Shrine tradition says that in year 40 of {{ill|lt=Emperor Keikō|Q329723|WD=Q329723}}, {{ill|lt=Yamato Takeru|Q461258|WD=Q461258}} enshrined his ancestral deity here during the eastern expedition, and in {{ill|lt=Hakuhō|Q11580612|WD=Q11580612}} 2 ({{ill|lt=673|Q31105|WD=Q31105}}) shrine buildings were dedicated to pray for good governance and peace under heaven. In the {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=register of deities}} of the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}}', completed in {{ill|lt=Enchō (era)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Enchō}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470}}), it is listed as "Sakaki Shrine" in {{ill|lt=Hanishina District|Q726661|WD=Q726661}}, {{ill|lt=Shinano Province|Q289250|WD=Q289250}}, and ranked as {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}. It was worshiped as the village shrine of Sakaki-gō, Hanishina District, and from the medieval period onward was called "Sakaki Ōmiya." It was revered by generations of the {{ill|lt=Murakami clan|Q11522833|WD=Q11522833}}, lords of {{ill|lt=Katsurao Castle|Q11620930|WD=Q11620930}}, and the site includes Mansen-ji, the remains of the Murakami residence, adjacent to the precincts. Katsurao Castle fell to the {{ill|lt=Takeda clan|Q1210114|WD=Q1210114}} in {{ill|lt=Tenbun (era)|Q1192853|WD=Q1192853|lt=Tenbun}} 22 ({{ill|lt=1553|Q6568|WD=Q6568}}), but in {{ill|lt=Eiroku|Q1143341|WD=Q1143341}} 11 ({{ill|lt=1568|Q6648|WD=Q6648}}), {{ill|lt=Takeda Shingen|Q276404|WD=Q276404}} donated shrine lands and revived it, and {{ill|lt=Takeda Katsuyori|Q662482|WD=Q662482}} also guaranteed those lands in {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058}} 7 ({{ill|lt=1579|Q6709|WD=Q6709}}). It is also said to have been greatly revered by magistrates of the {{ill|lt=Nakanojō Jinya|Q21653911|WD=Q21653911}} in the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963}}. === Precinct === File:Sakaki-jinja (Sakaki), honden.jpg|Honden File:Sakaki-jinja (Sakaki), haiden.jpg|Haiden File:Sakaki-jinja (Sakaki), komainu-2.jpg|Komainu (A), Sakaki Town designated cultural property File:Sakaki-jinja (Sakaki), komainu-3.jpg|Komainu (Un), Sakaki Town designated cultural property File:Sakaki-jinja (Sakaki), torii.jpg|Precinct torii File:Sakaki-jinja (Sakaki), entrance.jpg|Precinct entrance === Cultural properties === === Sakaki Town designated cultural properties === * Tangible cultural properties ** Wooden Kishin mask (sculpture) - designated October 1, 1984 (Shōwa 59). ** Wooden lion head (sculpture) - designated October 1, 1984 (Shōwa 59). ** One pair of Sakaki Shrine komainu (sculpture) - designated April 1, 1999 (Heisei 11). ** Four old documents of Sakaki Shrine (old documents) - donation letter of shrine lands by Takeda Shingen, guarantee letter by Takeda Katsuyori, prohibition by Hasegawa Yasazaemon, donation letter of sacred lands by Murakami Yoshikiyo. Designated October 1, 1984 (Shōwa 59). === Bibliography === *'Shinshu Culture Series: Temples and Shrines,' 1981 {{ill|lt=Shinano Mainichi Shimbun|Q11386181|WD=Q11386181|lt=Shinano Mainichi Shimbun Co.}} *Nagano Prefectural Shrine Association (supervision), 'Rituals of Shinshu,' Ginga Shobō, 1990 *Edited by {{ill|lt=Kenichi Tanigawa|Q2392596|WD=Q2392596}}, 'Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Places - 9: Mino, Hida, Shinano,' 2000 {{ill|lt=Hakusuisha|Q4346470|WD=Q4346470}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === * {{Commonscat-inline|Sakaki-jinja (Sakaki)}} {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=長野県}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:さかきしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sakaki Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sakaki Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shinano Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:長野県の神社]] [[Category:坂城町の建築物]] [[Category:坂城町の歴史]] [[Category:信濃国の式内社]] [[Category:長野県の旧県社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

== name in kana (P1814) == * さかきじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 4100005004825 == Commons category (P373) == * Sakaki-jinja (Sakaki) == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '長野県埴科郡坂城町坂城1205', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Sakaki-jinja (Sakaki)}} [[JA:坂城神社]] {{wikidata link|Q106852481}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Sakaki Shrine |画像 = [[File:Sakaki-jinja (Sakaki), keidai.jpg|250px]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Nagano Prefecture|Q127877|WD=Q127877|1=Nagano Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Hanishina District|Q726661|WD=Q726661|1=Hanishina District, Nagano}}{{ill|lt=Sakaki|Q1348962|WD=Q1348962|1=Sakaki, Nagano}}Sakaki 1205 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} |創建 = (trad.) year 40 of 12th emperor {{ill|lt=Emperor Keikō|Q329723|WD=Q329723|1=Emperor Keikō}} |本殿 = |別名 = Sakaki Ōmiya |札所等 = |例祭 = Third Sunday of April (spring festival)
Fourth Sunday of September (autumn festival) |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nagano|label=Sakaki Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Sakaki-jinja (Sakaki), otorii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Great torii}}]] '''Sakaki Shrine''' (さかきじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Sakaki, {{ill|lt=Sakaki|Q1348962|WD=Q1348962|1=Sakaki, Nagano}}, {{ill|lt=Hanishina District|Q726661|WD=Q726661|1=Hanishina District, Nagano}}, {{ill|lt=Nagano Prefecture|Q127877|WD=Q127877|1=Nagano Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former rank in the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}. === Overview === The main deity is {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}}. Shrine tradition says that in year 40 of {{ill|lt=Emperor Keikō|Q329723|WD=Q329723|1=Emperor Keikō}}, {{ill|lt=Yamato Takeru|Q461258|WD=Q461258|1=Yamato Takeru}} enshrined his ancestral deity here during the eastern expedition, and in {{ill|lt=Hakuhō|Q11580612|WD=Q11580612|1=Hakuhō}} 2 ({{ill|lt=673|Q31105|WD=Q31105|1=673}}) shrine buildings were dedicated to pray for good governance and peace under heaven. In the {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=register of deities|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} of the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}', completed in {{ill|lt=Enchō (era)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Enchō|1=Enchō}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}}), it is listed as "Sakaki Shrine" in {{ill|lt=Hanishina District|Q726661|WD=Q726661|1=Hanishina District, Nagano}}, {{ill|lt=Shinano Province|Q289250|WD=Q289250|1=Shinano Province}}, and ranked as {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}. It was worshiped as the village shrine of Sakaki-gō, Hanishina District, and from the medieval period onward was called "Sakaki Ōmiya." It was revered by generations of the {{ill|lt=Murakami clan|Q11522833|WD=Q11522833|1=Murakami clan}}, lords of {{ill|lt=Katsurao Castle|Q11620930|WD=Q11620930|1=Katsurao Castle}}, and the site includes Mansen-ji, the remains of the Murakami residence, adjacent to the precincts. Katsurao Castle fell to the {{ill|lt=Takeda clan|Q1210114|WD=Q1210114|1=Takeda clan}} in {{ill|lt=Tenbun (era)|Q1192853|WD=Q1192853|lt=Tenbun|1=Tenbun}} 22 ({{ill|lt=1553|Q6568|WD=Q6568|1=1553}}), but in {{ill|lt=Eiroku|Q1143341|WD=Q1143341|1=Eiroku}} 11 ({{ill|lt=1568|Q6648|WD=Q6648|1=1568}}), {{ill|lt=Takeda Shingen|Q276404|WD=Q276404|1=Takeda Shingen}} donated shrine lands and revived it, and {{ill|lt=Takeda Katsuyori|Q662482|WD=Q662482|1=Takeda Katsuyori}} also guaranteed those lands in {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 7 ({{ill|lt=1579|Q6709|WD=Q6709|1=1579}}). It is also said to have been greatly revered by magistrates of the {{ill|lt=Nakanojō Jinya|Q21653911|WD=Q21653911|1=Nakanojō Jin'ya}} in the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}. === Precinct === File:Sakaki-jinja (Sakaki), honden.jpg|Honden File:Sakaki-jinja (Sakaki), haiden.jpg|Haiden File:Sakaki-jinja (Sakaki), komainu-2.jpg|Komainu (A), Sakaki Town designated cultural property File:Sakaki-jinja (Sakaki), komainu-3.jpg|Komainu (Un), Sakaki Town designated cultural property File:Sakaki-jinja (Sakaki), torii.jpg|Precinct torii File:Sakaki-jinja (Sakaki), entrance.jpg|Precinct entrance === Cultural properties === === Sakaki Town designated cultural properties === * Tangible cultural properties ** Wooden Kishin mask (sculpture) - designated October 1, 1984 (Shōwa 59). ** Wooden lion head (sculpture) - designated October 1, 1984 (Shōwa 59). ** One pair of Sakaki Shrine komainu (sculpture) - designated April 1, 1999 (Heisei 11). ** Four old documents of Sakaki Shrine (old documents) - donation letter of shrine lands by Takeda Shingen, guarantee letter by Takeda Katsuyori, prohibition by Hasegawa Yasazaemon, donation letter of sacred lands by Murakami Yoshikiyo. Designated October 1, 1984 (Shōwa 59). === Bibliography === *'Shinshu Culture Series: Temples and Shrines,' 1981 {{ill|lt=Shinano Mainichi Shimbun|Q11386181|WD=Q11386181|lt=Shinano Mainichi Shimbun Co.|1=Shinano Mainichi Shimbun}} *Nagano Prefectural Shrine Association (supervision), 'Rituals of Shinshu,' Ginga Shobō, 1990 *Edited by {{ill|lt=Kenichi Tanigawa|Q2392596|WD=Q2392596|1=Ken'ichi Tanigawa}}, 'Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Places - 9: Mino, Hida, Shinano,' 2000 {{ill|lt=Hakusuisha|Q4346470|WD=Q4346470|1=Hakusuisha Publishing}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === * {{Commonscat-inline|Sakaki-jinja (Sakaki)}} {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=長野県}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:さかきしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sakaki Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sakaki Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shinano Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:長野県の神社]] [[Category:坂城町の建築物]] [[Category:坂城町の歴史]] [[Category:信濃国の式内社]] [[Category:長野県の旧県社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

== name in kana (P1814) == * さかきじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 4100005004825 == Commons category (P373) == * Sakaki-jinja (Sakaki) == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '長野県埴科郡坂城町坂城1205', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Sakaki-jinja (Sakaki)}} [[JA:坂城神社]] {{wikidata link|Q106852481}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Sakaki Shrine |画像 = [[File:Sakaki-jinja (Sakaki), keidai.jpg|250px]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Nagano Prefecture|Q127877|WD=Q127877|1=Nagano Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Hanishina District|Q726661|WD=Q726661|1=Hanishina District, Nagano}}{{ill|lt=Sakaki|Q1348962|WD=Q1348962|1=Sakaki, Nagano}}Sakaki 1205 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} |創建 = (trad.) year 40 of 12th emperor {{ill|lt=Emperor Keikō|Q329723|WD=Q329723|1=Emperor Keikō}} |本殿 = |別名 = Sakaki Ōmiya |札所等 = |例祭 = Third Sunday of April (spring festival)
Fourth Sunday of September (autumn festival) |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nagano|label=Sakaki Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Sakaki-jinja (Sakaki), otorii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Great torii}}]] '''Sakaki Shrine''' (さかきじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Sakaki, {{ill|lt=Sakaki|Q1348962|WD=Q1348962|1=Sakaki, Nagano}}, {{ill|lt=Hanishina District|Q726661|WD=Q726661|1=Hanishina District, Nagano}}, {{ill|lt=Nagano Prefecture|Q127877|WD=Q127877|1=Nagano Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former rank in the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}. === Overview === The main deity is {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}}. Shrine tradition says that in year 40 of {{ill|lt=Emperor Keikō|Q329723|WD=Q329723|1=Emperor Keikō}}, {{ill|lt=Yamato Takeru|Q461258|WD=Q461258|1=Yamato Takeru}} enshrined his ancestral deity here during the eastern expedition, and in {{ill|lt=Hakuhō|Q11580612|WD=Q11580612|1=Hakuhō}} 2 ({{ill|lt=673|Q31105|WD=Q31105|1=673}}) shrine buildings were dedicated to pray for good governance and peace under heaven. In the {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=register of deities|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} of the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}', completed in {{ill|lt=Enchō (era)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Enchō|1=Enchō}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}}), it is listed as "Sakaki Shrine" in {{ill|lt=Hanishina District|Q726661|WD=Q726661|1=Hanishina District, Nagano}}, {{ill|lt=Shinano Province|Q289250|WD=Q289250|1=Shinano Province}}, and ranked as {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}. It was worshiped as the village shrine of Sakaki-gō, Hanishina District, and from the medieval period onward was called "Sakaki Ōmiya." It was revered by generations of the {{ill|lt=Murakami clan|Q11522833|WD=Q11522833|1=Murakami clan}}, lords of {{ill|lt=Katsurao Castle|Q11620930|WD=Q11620930|1=Katsurao Castle}}, and the site includes Mansen-ji, the remains of the Murakami residence, adjacent to the precincts. Katsurao Castle fell to the {{ill|lt=Takeda clan|Q1210114|WD=Q1210114|1=Takeda clan}} in {{ill|lt=Tenbun (era)|Q1192853|WD=Q1192853|lt=Tenbun|1=Tenbun}} 22 ({{ill|lt=1553|Q6568|WD=Q6568|1=1553}}), but in {{ill|lt=Eiroku|Q1143341|WD=Q1143341|1=Eiroku}} 11 ({{ill|lt=1568|Q6648|WD=Q6648|1=1568}}), {{ill|lt=Takeda Shingen|Q276404|WD=Q276404|1=Takeda Shingen}} donated shrine lands and revived it, and {{ill|lt=Takeda Katsuyori|Q662482|WD=Q662482|1=Takeda Katsuyori}} also guaranteed those lands in {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 7 ({{ill|lt=1579|Q6709|WD=Q6709|1=1579}}). It is also said to have been greatly revered by magistrates of the {{ill|lt=Nakanojō Jinya|Q21653911|WD=Q21653911|1=Nakanojō Jin'ya}} in the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}. === Precinct === File:Sakaki-jinja (Sakaki), honden.jpg|Honden File:Sakaki-jinja (Sakaki), haiden.jpg|Haiden File:Sakaki-jinja (Sakaki), komainu-2.jpg|Komainu (A), Sakaki Town designated cultural property File:Sakaki-jinja (Sakaki), komainu-3.jpg|Komainu (Un), Sakaki Town designated cultural property File:Sakaki-jinja (Sakaki), torii.jpg|Precinct torii File:Sakaki-jinja (Sakaki), entrance.jpg|Precinct entrance === Cultural properties === === Sakaki Town designated cultural properties === * Tangible cultural properties ** Wooden Kishin mask (sculpture) - designated October 1, 1984 (Shōwa 59). ** Wooden lion head (sculpture) - designated October 1, 1984 (Shōwa 59). ** One pair of Sakaki Shrine komainu (sculpture) - designated April 1, 1999 (Heisei 11). ** Four old documents of Sakaki Shrine (old documents) - donation letter of shrine lands by Takeda Shingen, guarantee letter by Takeda Katsuyori, prohibition by Hasegawa Yasazaemon, donation letter of sacred lands by Murakami Yoshikiyo. Designated October 1, 1984 (Shōwa 59). === Bibliography === *'Shinshu Culture Series: Temples and Shrines,' 1981 {{ill|lt=Shinano Mainichi Shimbun|Q11386181|WD=Q11386181|lt=Shinano Mainichi Shimbun Co.|1=Shinano Mainichi Shimbun}} *Nagano Prefectural Shrine Association (supervision), 'Rituals of Shinshu,' Ginga Shobō, 1990 *Edited by {{ill|lt=Kenichi Tanigawa|Q2392596|WD=Q2392596|1=Ken'ichi Tanigawa}}, 'Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Places - 9: Mino, Hida, Shinano,' 2000 {{ill|lt=Hakusuisha|Q4346470|WD=Q4346470|1=Hakusuisha Publishing}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === * {{Commonscat-inline|Sakaki-jinja (Sakaki)}} {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=長野県}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:さかきしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sakaki Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sakaki Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shinano Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:長野県の神社]] [[Category:坂城町の建築物]] [[Category:坂城町の歴史]] [[Category:信濃国の式内社]] [[Category:長野県の旧県社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

== name in kana (P1814) == * さかきじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 4100005004825 == Commons category (P373) == * Sakaki-jinja (Sakaki) == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '長野県埴科郡坂城町坂城1205', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Sakaki-jinja (Sakaki)}} [[JA:坂城神社]] {{wikidata link|Q106852481}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Sakaki Shrine |画像 = [[File:Sakaki-jinja (Sakaki), keidai.jpg|250px]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Nagano Prefecture|Q127877|WD=Q127877|1=Nagano Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Hanishina District|Q726661|WD=Q726661|1=Hanishina District, Nagano}}{{ill|lt=Sakaki|Q1348962|WD=Q1348962|1=Sakaki, Nagano}}Sakaki 1205 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} |創建 = (trad.) year 40 of 12th emperor {{ill|lt=Emperor Keikō|Q329723|WD=Q329723|1=Emperor Keikō}} |本殿 = |別名 = Sakaki Ōmiya |札所等 = |例祭 = Third Sunday of April (spring festival)
Fourth Sunday of September (autumn festival) |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nagano|label=Sakaki Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Sakaki-jinja (Sakaki), otorii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Great torii}}]] '''Sakaki Shrine''' (さかきじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Sakaki, {{ill|lt=Sakaki|Q1348962|WD=Q1348962|1=Sakaki, Nagano}}, {{ill|lt=Hanishina District|Q726661|WD=Q726661|1=Hanishina District, Nagano}}, {{ill|lt=Nagano Prefecture|Q127877|WD=Q127877|1=Nagano Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former rank in the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}. === Overview === The main deity is {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}}. Shrine tradition says that in year 40 of {{ill|lt=Emperor Keikō|Q329723|WD=Q329723|1=Emperor Keikō}}, {{ill|lt=Yamato Takeru|Q461258|WD=Q461258|1=Yamato Takeru}} enshrined his ancestral deity here during the eastern expedition, and in {{ill|lt=Hakuhō|Q11580612|WD=Q11580612|1=Hakuhō}} 2 ({{ill|lt=673|Q31105|WD=Q31105|1=673}}) shrine buildings were dedicated to pray for good governance and peace under heaven. In the {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=register of deities|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} of the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}', completed in {{ill|lt=Enchō (era)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Enchō|1=Enchō}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}}), it is listed as "Sakaki Shrine" in {{ill|lt=Hanishina District|Q726661|WD=Q726661|1=Hanishina District, Nagano}}, {{ill|lt=Shinano Province|Q289250|WD=Q289250|1=Shinano Province}}, and ranked as {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}. It was worshiped as the village shrine of Sakaki-gō, Hanishina District, and from the medieval period onward was called "Sakaki Ōmiya." It was revered by generations of the {{ill|lt=Murakami clan|Q11522833|WD=Q11522833|1=Murakami clan}}, lords of {{ill|lt=Katsurao Castle|Q11620930|WD=Q11620930|1=Katsurao Castle}}, and the site includes Mansen-ji, the remains of the Murakami residence, adjacent to the precincts. Katsurao Castle fell to the {{ill|lt=Takeda clan|Q1210114|WD=Q1210114|1=Takeda clan}} in {{ill|lt=Tenbun (era)|Q1192853|WD=Q1192853|lt=Tenbun|1=Tenbun}} 22 ({{ill|lt=1553|Q6568|WD=Q6568|1=1553}}), but in {{ill|lt=Eiroku|Q1143341|WD=Q1143341|1=Eiroku}} 11 ({{ill|lt=1568|Q6648|WD=Q6648|1=1568}}), {{ill|lt=Takeda Shingen|Q276404|WD=Q276404|1=Takeda Shingen}} donated shrine lands and revived it, and {{ill|lt=Takeda Katsuyori|Q662482|WD=Q662482|1=Takeda Katsuyori}} also guaranteed those lands in {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 7 ({{ill|lt=1579|Q6709|WD=Q6709|1=1579}}). It is also said to have been greatly revered by magistrates of the {{ill|lt=Nakanojō Jinya|Q21653911|WD=Q21653911|1=Nakanojō Jin'ya}} in the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}. === Precinct === File:Sakaki-jinja (Sakaki), honden.jpg|Honden File:Sakaki-jinja (Sakaki), haiden.jpg|Haiden File:Sakaki-jinja (Sakaki), komainu-2.jpg|Komainu (A), Sakaki Town designated cultural property File:Sakaki-jinja (Sakaki), komainu-3.jpg|Komainu (Un), Sakaki Town designated cultural property File:Sakaki-jinja (Sakaki), torii.jpg|Precinct torii File:Sakaki-jinja (Sakaki), entrance.jpg|Precinct entrance === Cultural properties === === Sakaki Town designated cultural properties === * Tangible cultural properties ** Wooden Kishin mask (sculpture) - designated October 1, 1984 (Shōwa 59). ** Wooden lion head (sculpture) - designated October 1, 1984 (Shōwa 59). ** One pair of Sakaki Shrine komainu (sculpture) - designated April 1, 1999 (Heisei 11). ** Four old documents of Sakaki Shrine (old documents) - donation letter of shrine lands by Takeda Shingen, guarantee letter by Takeda Katsuyori, prohibition by Hasegawa Yasazaemon, donation letter of sacred lands by Murakami Yoshikiyo. Designated October 1, 1984 (Shōwa 59). === Bibliography === *'Shinshu Culture Series: Temples and Shrines,' 1981 {{ill|lt=Shinano Mainichi Shimbun|Q11386181|WD=Q11386181|lt=Shinano Mainichi Shimbun Co.|1=Shinano Mainichi Shimbun}} *Nagano Prefectural Shrine Association (supervision), 'Rituals of Shinshu,' Ginga Shobō, 1990 *Edited by {{ill|lt=Kenichi Tanigawa|Q2392596|WD=Q2392596|1=Ken'ichi Tanigawa}}, 'Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Places - 9: Mino, Hida, Shinano,' 2000 {{ill|lt=Hakusuisha|Q4346470|WD=Q4346470|1=Hakusuisha Publishing}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === * {{Commonscat-inline|Sakaki-jinja (Sakaki)}} {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=長野県}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:さかきしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sakaki Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sakaki Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shinano Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:長野県の神社]] [[Category:坂城町の建築物]] [[Category:坂城町の歴史]] [[Category:信濃国の式内社]] [[Category:長野県の旧県社]][[Category:qq]]

== name in kana (P1814) == * さかきじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 4100005004825 == Commons category (P373) == * Sakaki-jinja (Sakaki) == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '長野県埴科郡坂城町坂城1205', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Sakaki-jinja (Sakaki)}} [[JA:坂城神社]] {{wikidata link|Q106852481}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Sakaki Shrine |画像 = [[File:Sakaki-jinja (Sakaki), keidai.jpg|250px]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Nagano Prefecture|Q127877|WD=Q127877|1=Nagano Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Hanishina District|Q726661|WD=Q726661|1=Hanishina District, Nagano}}{{ill|lt=Sakaki|Q1348962|WD=Q1348962|1=Sakaki, Nagano}}Sakaki 1205 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} |創建 = (trad.) year 40 of 12th emperor {{ill|lt=Emperor Keikō|Q329723|WD=Q329723|1=Emperor Keikō}} |本殿 = |別名 = Sakaki Ōmiya |札所等 = |例祭 = Third Sunday of April (spring festival)
Fourth Sunday of September (autumn festival) |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nagano|label=Sakaki Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Sakaki-jinja (Sakaki), otorii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Great torii}}]] '''Sakaki Shrine''' (さかきじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Sakaki, {{ill|lt=Sakaki|Q1348962|WD=Q1348962|1=Sakaki, Nagano}}, {{ill|lt=Hanishina District|Q726661|WD=Q726661|1=Hanishina District, Nagano}}, {{ill|lt=Nagano Prefecture|Q127877|WD=Q127877|1=Nagano Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former rank in the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}. === Overview === The main deity is {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}}. Shrine tradition says that in year 40 of {{ill|lt=Emperor Keikō|Q329723|WD=Q329723|1=Emperor Keikō}}, {{ill|lt=Yamato Takeru|Q461258|WD=Q461258|1=Yamato Takeru}} enshrined his ancestral deity here during the eastern expedition, and in {{ill|lt=Hakuhō|Q11580612|WD=Q11580612|1=Hakuhō}} 2 ({{ill|lt=673|Q31105|WD=Q31105|1=673}}) shrine buildings were dedicated to pray for good governance and peace under heaven. In the {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=register of deities|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} of the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}', completed in {{ill|lt=Enchō (era)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Enchō|1=Enchō}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}}), it is listed as "Sakaki Shrine" in {{ill|lt=Hanishina District|Q726661|WD=Q726661|1=Hanishina District, Nagano}}, {{ill|lt=Shinano Province|Q289250|WD=Q289250|1=Shinano Province}}, and ranked as {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}. It was worshiped as the village shrine of Sakaki-gō, Hanishina District, and from the medieval period onward was called "Sakaki Ōmiya." It was revered by generations of the {{ill|lt=Murakami clan|Q11522833|WD=Q11522833|1=Murakami clan}}, lords of {{ill|lt=Katsurao Castle|Q11620930|WD=Q11620930|1=Katsurao Castle}}, and the site includes Mansen-ji, the remains of the Murakami residence, adjacent to the precincts. Katsurao Castle fell to the {{ill|lt=Takeda clan|Q1210114|WD=Q1210114|1=Takeda clan}} in {{ill|lt=Tenbun (era)|Q1192853|WD=Q1192853|lt=Tenbun|1=Tenbun}} 22 ({{ill|lt=1553|Q6568|WD=Q6568|1=1553}}), but in {{ill|lt=Eiroku|Q1143341|WD=Q1143341|1=Eiroku}} 11 ({{ill|lt=1568|Q6648|WD=Q6648|1=1568}}), {{ill|lt=Takeda Shingen|Q276404|WD=Q276404|1=Takeda Shingen}} donated shrine lands and revived it, and {{ill|lt=Takeda Katsuyori|Q662482|WD=Q662482|1=Takeda Katsuyori}} also guaranteed those lands in {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 7 ({{ill|lt=1579|Q6709|WD=Q6709|1=1579}}). It is also said to have been greatly revered by magistrates of the {{ill|lt=Nakanojō Jinya|Q21653911|WD=Q21653911|1=Nakanojō Jin'ya}} in the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}. === Precinct === File:Sakaki-jinja (Sakaki), honden.jpg|Honden File:Sakaki-jinja (Sakaki), haiden.jpg|Haiden File:Sakaki-jinja (Sakaki), komainu-2.jpg|Komainu (A), Sakaki Town designated cultural property File:Sakaki-jinja (Sakaki), komainu-3.jpg|Komainu (Un), Sakaki Town designated cultural property File:Sakaki-jinja (Sakaki), torii.jpg|Precinct torii File:Sakaki-jinja (Sakaki), entrance.jpg|Precinct entrance === Cultural properties === === Sakaki Town designated cultural properties === * Tangible cultural properties ** Wooden Kishin mask (sculpture) - designated October 1, 1984 (Shōwa 59). ** Wooden lion head (sculpture) - designated October 1, 1984 (Shōwa 59). ** One pair of Sakaki Shrine komainu (sculpture) - designated April 1, 1999 (Heisei 11). ** Four old documents of Sakaki Shrine (old documents) - donation letter of shrine lands by Takeda Shingen, guarantee letter by Takeda Katsuyori, prohibition by Hasegawa Yasazaemon, donation letter of sacred lands by Murakami Yoshikiyo. Designated October 1, 1984 (Shōwa 59). === Bibliography === *'Shinshu Culture Series: Temples and Shrines,' 1981 {{ill|lt=Shinano Mainichi Shimbun|Q11386181|WD=Q11386181|lt=Shinano Mainichi Shimbun Co.|1=Shinano Mainichi Shimbun}} *Nagano Prefectural Shrine Association (supervision), 'Rituals of Shinshu,' Ginga Shobō, 1990 *Edited by {{ill|lt=Kenichi Tanigawa|Q2392596|WD=Q2392596|1=Ken'ichi Tanigawa}}, 'Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Places - 9: Mino, Hida, Shinano,' 2000 {{ill|lt=Hakusuisha|Q4346470|WD=Q4346470|1=Hakusuisha Publishing}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === * {{Commonscat-inline|Sakaki-jinja (Sakaki)}} {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=長野県}} {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sakaki Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sakaki Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shinano Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:長野県の神社]] [[Category:坂城町の建築物]] [[Category:坂城町の歴史]] [[Category:信濃国の式内社]] [[Category:長野県の旧県社]][[Category:qq]]

== name in kana (P1814) == * さかきじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 4100005004825 == Commons category (P373) == * Sakaki-jinja (Sakaki) == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '長野県埴科郡坂城町坂城1205', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Sakaki-jinja (Sakaki)}} [[JA:坂城神社]] {{wikidata link|Q106852481}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Sakaki Shrine |画像 = [[File:Sakaki-jinja (Sakaki), keidai.jpg|250px]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Nagano Prefecture|Q127877|WD=Q127877|1=Nagano Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Hanishina District|Q726661|WD=Q726661|1=Hanishina District, Nagano}}{{ill|lt=Sakaki|Q1348962|WD=Q1348962|1=Sakaki, Nagano}}Sakaki 1205 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} |創建 = (trad.) year 40 of 12th emperor {{ill|lt=Emperor Keikō|Q329723|WD=Q329723|1=Emperor Keikō}} |本殿 = |別名 = Sakaki Ōmiya |札所等 = |例祭 = Third Sunday of April (spring festival)
Fourth Sunday of September (autumn festival) |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nagano|label=Sakaki Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Sakaki-jinja (Sakaki), otorii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Great torii}}]] '''Sakaki Shrine''' (さかきじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Sakaki, {{ill|lt=Sakaki|Q1348962|WD=Q1348962|1=Sakaki, Nagano}}, {{ill|lt=Hanishina District|Q726661|WD=Q726661|1=Hanishina District, Nagano}}, {{ill|lt=Nagano Prefecture|Q127877|WD=Q127877|1=Nagano Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former rank in the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}. === Overview === The main deity is {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}}. Shrine tradition says that in year 40 of {{ill|lt=Emperor Keikō|Q329723|WD=Q329723|1=Emperor Keikō}}, {{ill|lt=Yamato Takeru|Q461258|WD=Q461258|1=Yamato Takeru}} enshrined his ancestral deity here during the eastern expedition, and in {{ill|lt=Hakuhō|Q11580612|WD=Q11580612|1=Hakuhō}} 2 ({{ill|lt=673|Q31105|WD=Q31105|1=673}}) shrine buildings were dedicated to pray for good governance and peace under heaven. In the {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=register of deities|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} of the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}', completed in {{ill|lt=Enchō (era)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Enchō|1=Enchō}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}}), it is listed as "Sakaki Shrine" in {{ill|lt=Hanishina District|Q726661|WD=Q726661|1=Hanishina District, Nagano}}, {{ill|lt=Shinano Province|Q289250|WD=Q289250|1=Shinano Province}}, and ranked as {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}. It was worshiped as the village shrine of Sakaki-gō, Hanishina District, and from the medieval period onward was called "Sakaki Ōmiya." It was revered by generations of the {{ill|lt=Murakami clan|Q11522833|WD=Q11522833|1=Murakami clan}}, lords of {{ill|lt=Katsurao Castle|Q11620930|WD=Q11620930|1=Katsurao Castle}}, and the site includes Mansen-ji, the remains of the Murakami residence, adjacent to the precincts. Katsurao Castle fell to the {{ill|lt=Takeda clan|Q1210114|WD=Q1210114|1=Takeda clan}} in {{ill|lt=Tenbun (era)|Q1192853|WD=Q1192853|lt=Tenbun|1=Tenbun}} 22 ({{ill|lt=1553|Q6568|WD=Q6568|1=1553}}), but in {{ill|lt=Eiroku|Q1143341|WD=Q1143341|1=Eiroku}} 11 ({{ill|lt=1568|Q6648|WD=Q6648|1=1568}}), {{ill|lt=Takeda Shingen|Q276404|WD=Q276404|1=Takeda Shingen}} donated shrine lands and revived it, and {{ill|lt=Takeda Katsuyori|Q662482|WD=Q662482|1=Takeda Katsuyori}} also guaranteed those lands in {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 7 ({{ill|lt=1579|Q6709|WD=Q6709|1=1579}}). It is also said to have been greatly revered by magistrates of the {{ill|lt=Nakanojō Jinya|Q21653911|WD=Q21653911|1=Nakanojō Jin'ya}} in the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}. === Precinct === File:Sakaki-jinja (Sakaki), honden.jpg|Honden File:Sakaki-jinja (Sakaki), haiden.jpg|Haiden File:Sakaki-jinja (Sakaki), komainu-2.jpg|Komainu (A), Sakaki Town designated cultural property File:Sakaki-jinja (Sakaki), komainu-3.jpg|Komainu (Un), Sakaki Town designated cultural property File:Sakaki-jinja (Sakaki), torii.jpg|Precinct torii File:Sakaki-jinja (Sakaki), entrance.jpg|Precinct entrance === Cultural properties === === Sakaki Town designated cultural properties === * Tangible cultural properties ** Wooden Kishin mask (sculpture) - designated October 1, 1984 (Shōwa 59). ** Wooden lion head (sculpture) - designated October 1, 1984 (Shōwa 59). ** One pair of Sakaki Shrine komainu (sculpture) - designated April 1, 1999 (Heisei 11). ** Four old documents of Sakaki Shrine (old documents) - donation letter of shrine lands by Takeda Shingen, guarantee letter by Takeda Katsuyori, prohibition by Hasegawa Yasazaemon, donation letter of sacred lands by Murakami Yoshikiyo. Designated October 1, 1984 (Shōwa 59). === Bibliography === *'Shinshu Culture Series: Temples and Shrines,' 1981 {{ill|lt=Shinano Mainichi Shimbun|Q11386181|WD=Q11386181|lt=Shinano Mainichi Shimbun Co.|1=Shinano Mainichi Shimbun}} *Nagano Prefectural Shrine Association (supervision), 'Rituals of Shinshu,' Ginga Shobō, 1990 *Edited by {{ill|lt=Kenichi Tanigawa|Q2392596|WD=Q2392596|1=Ken'ichi Tanigawa}}, 'Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Places - 9: Mino, Hida, Shinano,' 2000 {{ill|lt=Hakusuisha|Q4346470|WD=Q4346470|1=Hakusuisha Publishing}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === * {{Commonscat-inline|Sakaki-jinja (Sakaki)}} {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=長野県}} {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sakaki Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sakaki Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shinano Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:長野県の神社]] [[Category:坂城町の建築物]] [[Category:坂城町の歴史]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shinano Province]] [[Category:長野県の旧県社]][[Category:qq]]

== name in kana (P1814) == * さかきじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 4100005004825 == Commons category (P373) == * Sakaki-jinja (Sakaki) == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '長野県埴科郡坂城町坂城1205', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Sakaki-jinja (Sakaki)}} [[JA:坂城神社]] {{wikidata link|Q106852481}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Sakaki Shrine |画像 = [[File:Sakaki-jinja (Sakaki), keidai.jpg|250px]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Nagano Prefecture|Q127877|WD=Q127877|1=Nagano Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Hanishina District|Q726661|WD=Q726661|1=Hanishina District, Nagano}}{{ill|lt=Sakaki|Q1348962|WD=Q1348962|1=Sakaki, Nagano}}Sakaki 1205 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} |創建 = (trad.) year 40 of 12th emperor {{ill|lt=Emperor Keikō|Q329723|WD=Q329723|1=Emperor Keikō}} |本殿 = |別名 = Sakaki Ōmiya |札所等 = |例祭 = Third Sunday of April (spring festival)
Fourth Sunday of September (autumn festival) |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nagano|label=Sakaki Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Sakaki-jinja (Sakaki), otorii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Great torii}}]] '''Sakaki Shrine''' (さかきじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Sakaki, {{ill|lt=Sakaki|Q1348962|WD=Q1348962|1=Sakaki, Nagano}}, {{ill|lt=Hanishina District|Q726661|WD=Q726661|1=Hanishina District, Nagano}}, {{ill|lt=Nagano Prefecture|Q127877|WD=Q127877|1=Nagano Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former rank in the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}. === Overview === The main deity is {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}}. Shrine tradition says that in year 40 of {{ill|lt=Emperor Keikō|Q329723|WD=Q329723|1=Emperor Keikō}}, {{ill|lt=Yamato Takeru|Q461258|WD=Q461258|1=Yamato Takeru}} enshrined his ancestral deity here during the eastern expedition, and in {{ill|lt=Hakuhō|Q11580612|WD=Q11580612|1=Hakuhō}} 2 ({{ill|lt=673|Q31105|WD=Q31105|1=673}}) shrine buildings were dedicated to pray for good governance and peace under heaven. In the {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=register of deities|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} of the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}', completed in {{ill|lt=Enchō (era)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Enchō|1=Enchō}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}}), it is listed as "Sakaki Shrine" in {{ill|lt=Hanishina District|Q726661|WD=Q726661|1=Hanishina District, Nagano}}, {{ill|lt=Shinano Province|Q289250|WD=Q289250|1=Shinano Province}}, and ranked as {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}. It was worshiped as the village shrine of Sakaki-gō, Hanishina District, and from the medieval period onward was called "Sakaki Ōmiya." It was revered by generations of the {{ill|lt=Murakami clan|Q11522833|WD=Q11522833|1=Murakami clan}}, lords of {{ill|lt=Katsurao Castle|Q11620930|WD=Q11620930|1=Katsurao Castle}}, and the site includes Mansen-ji, the remains of the Murakami residence, adjacent to the precincts. Katsurao Castle fell to the {{ill|lt=Takeda clan|Q1210114|WD=Q1210114|1=Takeda clan}} in {{ill|lt=Tenbun (era)|Q1192853|WD=Q1192853|lt=Tenbun|1=Tenbun}} 22 ({{ill|lt=1553|Q6568|WD=Q6568|1=1553}}), but in {{ill|lt=Eiroku|Q1143341|WD=Q1143341|1=Eiroku}} 11 ({{ill|lt=1568|Q6648|WD=Q6648|1=1568}}), {{ill|lt=Takeda Shingen|Q276404|WD=Q276404|1=Takeda Shingen}} donated shrine lands and revived it, and {{ill|lt=Takeda Katsuyori|Q662482|WD=Q662482|1=Takeda Katsuyori}} also guaranteed those lands in {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 7 ({{ill|lt=1579|Q6709|WD=Q6709|1=1579}}). It is also said to have been greatly revered by magistrates of the {{ill|lt=Nakanojō Jinya|Q21653911|WD=Q21653911|1=Nakanojō Jin'ya}} in the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}. === Precinct === File:Sakaki-jinja (Sakaki), honden.jpg|Honden File:Sakaki-jinja (Sakaki), haiden.jpg|Haiden File:Sakaki-jinja (Sakaki), komainu-2.jpg|Komainu (A), Sakaki Town designated cultural property File:Sakaki-jinja (Sakaki), komainu-3.jpg|Komainu (Un), Sakaki Town designated cultural property File:Sakaki-jinja (Sakaki), torii.jpg|Precinct torii File:Sakaki-jinja (Sakaki), entrance.jpg|Precinct entrance === Cultural properties === === Sakaki Town designated cultural properties === * Tangible cultural properties ** Wooden Kishin mask (sculpture) - designated October 1, 1984 (Shōwa 59). ** Wooden lion head (sculpture) - designated October 1, 1984 (Shōwa 59). ** One pair of Sakaki Shrine komainu (sculpture) - designated April 1, 1999 (Heisei 11). ** Four old documents of Sakaki Shrine (old documents) - donation letter of shrine lands by Takeda Shingen, guarantee letter by Takeda Katsuyori, prohibition by Hasegawa Yasazaemon, donation letter of sacred lands by Murakami Yoshikiyo. Designated October 1, 1984 (Shōwa 59). === Bibliography === *'Shinshu Culture Series: Temples and Shrines,' 1981 {{ill|lt=Shinano Mainichi Shimbun|Q11386181|WD=Q11386181|lt=Shinano Mainichi Shimbun Co.|1=Shinano Mainichi Shimbun}} *Nagano Prefectural Shrine Association (supervision), 'Rituals of Shinshu,' Ginga Shobō, 1990 *Edited by {{ill|lt=Kenichi Tanigawa|Q2392596|WD=Q2392596|1=Ken'ichi Tanigawa}}, 'Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Places - 9: Mino, Hida, Shinano,' 2000 {{ill|lt=Hakusuisha|Q4346470|WD=Q4346470|1=Hakusuisha Publishing}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === * {{Commonscat-inline|Sakaki-jinja (Sakaki)}} {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=長野県}} {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sakaki Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sakaki Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shinano Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:長野県の神社]] [[Category:坂城町の建築物]] [[Category:坂城町の歴史]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shinano Province]] [[Category:長野県の旧県社]]

== name in kana (P1814) == * さかきじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 4100005004825 == Commons category (P373) == * Sakaki-jinja (Sakaki) == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '長野県埴科郡坂城町坂城1205', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Sakaki-jinja (Sakaki)}} [[JA:坂城神社]] {{wikidata link|Q106852481}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Sakaki Shrine |画像 = [[File:Sakaki-jinja (Sakaki), keidai.jpg|250px]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Nagano Prefecture|Q127877|WD=Q127877|1=Nagano Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Hanishina District|Q726661|WD=Q726661|1=Hanishina District, Nagano}}{{ill|lt=Sakaki|Q1348962|WD=Q1348962|1=Sakaki, Nagano}}Sakaki 1205 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} |創建 = (trad.) year 40 of 12th emperor {{ill|lt=Emperor Keikō|Q329723|WD=Q329723|1=Emperor Keikō}} |本殿 = |別名 = Sakaki Ōmiya |札所等 = |例祭 = Third Sunday of April (spring festival)
Fourth Sunday of September (autumn festival) |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nagano|label=Sakaki Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Sakaki-jinja (Sakaki), otorii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Great torii}}]] '''Sakaki Shrine''' (さかきじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Sakaki, {{ill|lt=Sakaki|Q1348962|WD=Q1348962|1=Sakaki, Nagano}}, {{ill|lt=Hanishina District|Q726661|WD=Q726661|1=Hanishina District, Nagano}}, {{ill|lt=Nagano Prefecture|Q127877|WD=Q127877|1=Nagano Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former rank in the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}. === Overview === The main deity is {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}}. Shrine tradition says that in year 40 of {{ill|lt=Emperor Keikō|Q329723|WD=Q329723|1=Emperor Keikō}}, {{ill|lt=Yamato Takeru|Q461258|WD=Q461258|1=Yamato Takeru}} enshrined his ancestral deity here during the eastern expedition, and in {{ill|lt=Hakuhō|Q11580612|WD=Q11580612|1=Hakuhō}} 2 ({{ill|lt=673|Q31105|WD=Q31105|1=673}}) shrine buildings were dedicated to pray for good governance and peace under heaven. In the {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=register of deities|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} of the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}', completed in {{ill|lt=Enchō (era)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Enchō|1=Enchō}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}}), it is listed as "Sakaki Shrine" in {{ill|lt=Hanishina District|Q726661|WD=Q726661|1=Hanishina District, Nagano}}, {{ill|lt=Shinano Province|Q289250|WD=Q289250|1=Shinano Province}}, and ranked as {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}. It was worshiped as the village shrine of Sakaki-gō, Hanishina District, and from the medieval period onward was called "Sakaki Ōmiya." It was revered by generations of the {{ill|lt=Murakami clan|Q11522833|WD=Q11522833|1=Murakami clan}}, lords of {{ill|lt=Katsurao Castle|Q11620930|WD=Q11620930|1=Katsurao Castle}}, and the site includes Mansen-ji, the remains of the Murakami residence, adjacent to the precincts. Katsurao Castle fell to the {{ill|lt=Takeda clan|Q1210114|WD=Q1210114|1=Takeda clan}} in {{ill|lt=Tenbun (era)|Q1192853|WD=Q1192853|lt=Tenbun|1=Tenbun}} 22 ({{ill|lt=1553|Q6568|WD=Q6568|1=1553}}), but in {{ill|lt=Eiroku|Q1143341|WD=Q1143341|1=Eiroku}} 11 ({{ill|lt=1568|Q6648|WD=Q6648|1=1568}}), {{ill|lt=Takeda Shingen|Q276404|WD=Q276404|1=Takeda Shingen}} donated shrine lands and revived it, and {{ill|lt=Takeda Katsuyori|Q662482|WD=Q662482|1=Takeda Katsuyori}} also guaranteed those lands in {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 7 ({{ill|lt=1579|Q6709|WD=Q6709|1=1579}}). It is also said to have been greatly revered by magistrates of the {{ill|lt=Nakanojō Jinya|Q21653911|WD=Q21653911|1=Nakanojō Jin'ya}} in the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}. === Precinct === File:Sakaki-jinja (Sakaki), honden.jpg|Honden File:Sakaki-jinja (Sakaki), haiden.jpg|Haiden File:Sakaki-jinja (Sakaki), komainu-2.jpg|Komainu (A), Sakaki Town designated cultural property File:Sakaki-jinja (Sakaki), komainu-3.jpg|Komainu (Un), Sakaki Town designated cultural property File:Sakaki-jinja (Sakaki), torii.jpg|Precinct torii File:Sakaki-jinja (Sakaki), entrance.jpg|Precinct entrance === Cultural properties === === Sakaki Town designated cultural properties === * Tangible cultural properties ** Wooden Kishin mask (sculpture) - designated October 1, 1984 (Shōwa 59). ** Wooden lion head (sculpture) - designated October 1, 1984 (Shōwa 59). ** One pair of Sakaki Shrine komainu (sculpture) - designated April 1, 1999 (Heisei 11). ** Four old documents of Sakaki Shrine (old documents) - donation letter of shrine lands by Takeda Shingen, guarantee letter by Takeda Katsuyori, prohibition by Hasegawa Yasazaemon, donation letter of sacred lands by Murakami Yoshikiyo. Designated October 1, 1984 (Shōwa 59). === Bibliography === *'Shinshu Culture Series: Temples and Shrines,' 1981 {{ill|lt=Shinano Mainichi Shimbun|Q11386181|WD=Q11386181|lt=Shinano Mainichi Shimbun Co.|1=Shinano Mainichi Shimbun}} *Nagano Prefectural Shrine Association (supervision), 'Rituals of Shinshu,' Ginga Shobō, 1990 *Edited by {{ill|lt=Kenichi Tanigawa|Q2392596|WD=Q2392596|1=Ken'ichi Tanigawa}}, 'Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Places - 9: Mino, Hida, Shinano,' 2000 {{ill|lt=Hakusuisha|Q4346470|WD=Q4346470|1=Hakusuisha Publishing}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === * {{Commonscat-inline|Sakaki-jinja (Sakaki)}} {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=長野県}} {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sakaki Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sakaki Shrine]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shinano Province]] [[Category:長野県の神社]] [[Category:坂城町の建築物]] [[Category:坂城町の歴史]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shinano Province]] [[Category:長野県の旧県社]]
